attribution_method: Exact linear attribution: contribution_i = coef_i * z_i (z standardized). Equals SHAP for a linear model with feature-independence baseline since E[z_i]=0; no shap library used.

production_refit.note: Refit on all 34 trucks for deployment/explanation only. Validation numbers are X2 nested-LOVO; resubstitution AUROC reported for transparency, never as a performance claim.

coefficients.physics_prior: rising within-week electrical noise = volatility drift (common channel of A1 solenoid, A2 battery, A3 instability)
falling engine-off rest-voltage floor = battery floor sagging vs own baseline (battery-cascade pathway, D section 3)

coefficients.verdict: FLAGGED: multivariate sign contradicts physics -- suppressor effect (feature is physics-consistent univariately but strongly correlated with a dominant feature; ridge assigns it a corrective negative weight)
coefficients.physics_prior: widening weekly drive-voltage envelope = regulation instability / electrical degradation
crank dips deepening vs own baseline = battery/cascade load signature (E2: A2 VINs dip_depth +1.1..+3.6 V)

physics_sign_check.note: Univariate direction is the physics test; a multivariate sign flip for a feature correlated r=+0.82 with the dominant feature is a ridge suppressor effect, not a physics contradiction.

calibration_bridge.purpose: maps production decision values to the OOF recalibrated probability scale for counterfactuals

recomputed_headline.auroc_note: official headline pooled on raw per-fold decision values (X2); `prob` is per-fold sigmoid-mapped, recomputation differs +0.002

tier_rule: GREEN < 0.35 <= AMBER < 0.55 <= RED on recalibrated probability

drivers.gloss: within-week voltage noise is 2.07x its own 40-week baseline -- electrical volatility is drifting up (volatility-drift pathway)

weekly voltage range widening at +0.200 V/wk over the last 12 weeks [model uses this term as a statistical suppressor for the correlated noise ratio (r=+0.82); its standalone physics direction is widening = risk -- see global coefficient table]
engine-off rest-voltage floor moved -3.15 V vs own baseline -- battery floor sagging, battery-cascade pathway
crank voltage dips +4.12 V deeper than own baseline in the last 90 d -- heavier cranking load / battery-cascade signature

cards_ordered_by_prob_recal_desc.counterfactual: would drop RED -> AMBER if within-week noise ratio fell by 1.06 x (ratio) (vsi_withinwk_std_ratio_30d_w: 2.07 -> 1.01), all else equal

drivers.gloss: within-week voltage noise at 0.45x own baseline -- quiet, fleet-typical electrical behaviour (protective)
rest-voltage floor +0.43 V vs own baseline -- battery floor stable/recovering (protective)

weekly voltage range trend +0.000 V/wk -- envelope flat [model uses this term as a statistical suppressor for the correlated noise ratio (r=+0.82); its standalone physics direction is widening = risk -- see global coefficient table]
crank voltage dips +0.86 V deeper than own baseline in the last 90 d -- heavier cranking load / battery-cascade signature
